content.full_text:
September 13, 1994


Honorable Ruben S. Ayala
Member of the Senate
P.O. Box 7177
San Bernardino, CA  92411

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                             Our File No. A-94-282

Dear Senator Ayala:

     You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

                              QUESTION

     You have asked if it is permissible for you to open a
campaign account at the Chino Valley Bank in light of the fact
that you own stock in the bank.

                             CONCLUSION

     There is nothing in the Act which would prohibit you from
opening a campaign account at the Chino Valley Bank.

                              FACTS

     During our telephone conversation of September 8, 1994, you
stated that you own stock in the Chino Valley Bank and that you
would like to move your campaign account to that bank.

                             ANALYSIS

     The Act requires that a candidate establish a campaign
account at an office of a financial institution located in the
state of California.  (Section 85201.)  The Act's campaign
provisions impose no other restrictions with regard to the

_________________________
1/     Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Honorable Ruben S. Ayala
Our File No. A-94-282
Page 2


financial institution a candidate may use for the purpose of
opening a campaign bank account.

     In addition, the conflict of interest provisions of the Act
require public officials to refrain from making or participating
in making any governmental decision which will have a foreseeable
material financial effect on an entity in which they have an
investment worth $1,000 or more. (Section 87100, et seq.)
Because the selection of a campaign bank account does not involve
the making of a governmental decision, the Act's conflict of
interest provisions would not be applicable to your question.

     If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel


                                        By:  Carla Wardlow
                                             Division Chief
                                             Technical Assistance Division

QUESTION: You have asked if it is permissible for you to open a
campaign account at the Chino Valley Bank in light of the fact
that you own stock in the bank.

CONCLUSION: There is nothing in the Act which would prohibit you from
opening a campaign account at the Chino Valley Bank.
